title: AI Agents Are Coming for Your Job (and That's Okay)
description: Three AI newsletters break down the agent revolution: what's real, what's hype, and why your career might be safer than you think.

title: The Market Rally Nobody Expected
description: Market Briefs and Morning Brew unpack a surprising rally driven by AI infrastructure spending and a Fed pivot nobody saw coming.

title: Sleep Science: What the Experts Actually Do
description: Huberman Lab and The Checkup reveal what sleep researchers practice in their own lives — and it's not what you'd expect.

title: Startup Funding Is Weird Right Now
description: Crunchbase Daily and StrictlyVC decode the paradox: record funding for AI, crickets for everything else.

title: The Productivity Trap: Why Doing Less Gets More Done
description: Actionable insights from productivity newsletters on why your optimization habit might be the thing holding you back.

title: Climate Tech's Billion-Dollar Moment
description: Three climate and energy newsletters converge on the same story: massive capital is finally flowing into carbon removal and grid storage.

title: The Longevity Playbook
description: Peter Attia, David Sinclair, and the latest research on what actually extends lifespan — from VO2 max to caloric restriction.

title: Why Everyone's Talking About Nuclear Energy
description: Big Tech needs 24/7 power for AI data centers. Nuclear is back, and this time the economics might actually work.

title: The Housing Market Makes No Sense
description: Prices keep rising, nobody can afford a house, and inventory is at historic lows. Three newsletters explain the paradox.

title: Remote Work Is Splitting in Two
description: The data is in: hybrid won. But the story is more complicated — and more political — than the headlines suggest.

title: What Ultra-Processed Food Does to Your Brain
description: The science is getting scary: UPF doesn't just affect your waistline, it rewires your brain's reward system and gut microbiome.

title: Robotics Just Had Its iPhone Moment
description: Humanoid robots are on factory floors, foundation models are teaching them to learn, and the $24 trillion market is wide open.

title: The Psychology of Money Mistakes
description: Morgan Housel, Kyla Scanlon, and The Financial Diet on why smart people make terrible financial decisions — and how to stop.

title: How to Actually Read More Books
description: Ryan Holiday, Austin Kleon, and James Clear share systems that work — and explain why your Goodreads goal is counterproductive.

title: Space Tech Is Booming (Quietly)
description: 200+ SpaceX launches, defense satellites, and a $500B commercial space economy. The space boom is real — and mostly under the radar.

title: The Parenting Advice Nobody Asked For
description: Emily Oster, Jonathan Haidt, and the free-range kids movement — data-driven takes on screens, anxiety, and letting go.

title: Why Your Gut Controls Your Mood
description: 95% of serotonin is made in your gut. Three science newsletters unpack the gut-brain axis and what to actually eat about it.

title: The Creator Economy Grew Up
description: The creator middle class is emerging, brands are ditching ads for partnerships, and boring creators are outearning influencers.
